text.title: api-monitoring-health-checks Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-monitoring-health-checks as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Configuring Kubernetes liveness probe to hit /readyz (which calls downstream dependencies).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If a downstream dependency is slow, the liveness probe fails, triggering container restarts. The pod restarts into the same degraded dependency, causing a restart loop that takes down the service even though the process itself is healthy.

failure-mode.description: Using a single /health endpoint for both liveness and readiness without distinguishing what it checks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forces a trade-off: either risk cascade restarts (if it checks downstream) or provide no signal about dependency health (if it doesn't). Both outcomes are worse than the split pattern.

failure-mode.description: Using invented or undocumented vendor health URLs like https://api.stripe.com/health in the readiness probe.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Undocumented URLs can return 404 at any time or not exist at all. The probe then always fails or produces false positives. Always verify health endpoint contracts from official vendor documentation.
